message:
fix(runtime): Add implicit exception context chaining

When raising an exception inside an except handler, Python automatically
sets __context__ to the exception being handled. This implements that
behavior by:

1. Adding context_exception_storage thread-local for storing the context
2. In getExceptionFull(), checking if there's an active exception on the
   stack and setting __context__ from it

This fixes assertIsInstance(e.__context__, TypeError) assertions that
were failing because __context__ was null.

Test status:
- test_exception_variations: 30/30 pass
- Core tests: 4/5 pass (bool __len__ is pre-existing)
